Why Ignoring Live Selling Would Be Irresponsible

the live selling model just to be completely honest and eBay knows this. We've had this conversation with them like it does not fit our traditional model and the way we operate. But at the same time, when you look at just like the macroeconomics of all marketplaces, not just collectibles, like live selling is accelerating so fast and growing so much, it would be professionally irresponsible of me to not say, "Hey, we got to go try this out. We got to learn what works. We got to find the right ways to do it." And so, you know, our first live stream, I made a ton of mistakes. Like, we didn't put the listings up until the day of. I Made $20,000 Buying and Grading One Card! 

Okay, the moment of truth is here. The grades are back. I'm back at the Dallas Card Show and today I'm here to make some money. I'm going to go look around the show floor for raw cards that I feel will grade well and I'm going to pick them up and then submit them for grading hopefully to make profit. Let's go. Okay, so it's actually going to be tricky. This is going to be tough for me today because this Dallas car show, you've got a lot of the biggest, most savvy dealers in the nation. And what that means is that a lot of them know all about grading raw cards for profit. So, the chance that I'm going to find a lot of valuable just raw cards sitting there that I could grade perfectly and make lots of money, it's going to be hard. But, I got some strategies. I got some theories. some tricky ways I might be able to get it done. So, I'm ready to put it to work. So, this dealer has a whole case full of these oncard autograph rookie cards of players like Ryan Bosworth and Dwight Clark and Marcus Allen. You got Billy Sims up here. Uh Mike Singleary rookie card. But if you notice all of these, none of them are card graded, right? They're all only graded on the autograph or in some cases they're just simply authentic without any type of card grade at all. If any of them are in really good condition, it could be an opportunity to get a dual grade and actually add some value to the card. This one actually kind of caught my eye cuz that's a Walter Payton and any Walter Peyton rookie or not rookie I should say, but any Walder Payton autograph can be valuable. Now, this card is just an authentic auto. The card itself is not graded at all. I do see like you can see some white on the edge of the card. So that's definitely not a candidate for like a a PSA 10 or anything of that nature. But I am curious, has this card ever been actually dual graded by PSA ever before? Or could it be could it be a situation where it ends up as like a pop one type situation which sometimes you'll find and then that can drive the value up. And if it has been dual graded by PSA before, what did that sell for compared to just the raw or just the, you know, autograph authenticated version of the card? Sometimes there can be a big price difference. So I might look around in my app here and see if any of these are worth picking up. All right, so I just looked up this Brian Bosworth card. So this is one where once again they they have only authenticated the autograph, not graded the card at all, but the card looks to be in quite good shape. And I can see here in market movers that a PSA 8 with a nine auto of this card recently sold for $115. He's got this version of the card stickered at $95. So that difference right there wouldn't be quite enough to even make up for the grading fee. But that's assuming it grades an eight. This card looks to be in better shape than an eight in my opinion. text:
TOPPS RUINS HOBBY BOXES!  2025 STADIUM CLUB NEW RELEASE PREVIEW!

Hey everybody, welcome back to another brand new release preview video. Today we've got the hobby boxes of 2025 Stadium Club. Probably the second best configuration of the three that are out there. You've got mega boxes, which we covered yesterday, hobbies today, and then I don't even know, maybe we'll do the blasters tomorrow, but the blasters are definitely the worst configuration. Let's tell you why you might want to even avoid a hobby box this time around, which is really weird for me to say. Typically, I'd usually advise you to go after the hobby because you get the guaranteed hit or hits in a hobby box. But, however, this time, surprisingly, there's less cards in a hobby box than in a mega box. You can see this little chart that I made yesterday. The MSRP on the mega boxes was 50. There's 144 cards packed in there. It's 35 cents a card. This box right here that now only has one autograph. We'll talk more about that in a minute. This only has eight cards per packs pack and what is it like 14 pack 16 packs times eight? So 128 cards and you're getting less cards and you're paying way more. It's a$12 a card. Essentially, you are paying. And by the way, these are all sold out at MSRP. So, if you can find it for 130, that's awesome. You got a really great local card shop. But if you go and buy this online, they're like 1701 180 right now. Today, they've come down a little bit in one day. But imagine paying say $170 for a box of these. You're paying $120 for one autograph and getting less cards. Man, that's an awful awful pro proposition. just absolutely terrible when you have the mega box out there. So, I just want to put that out there. If you didn't see yesterday, mega boxes are absolutely jam-packed with cards. If you just like stadium club because you like the photography and you want to put the set together, go buy a mega box or two. You could literally buy three mega boxes for the price of one hobby box and get over three times as many cards and probably get maybe two autographs out of those three boxes. You never know. No guarantee of an autograph in the Megas, but there is one guarantee of an auto in this, which is another big issue that I and everybody else have with this product. All the other years of Stadium Club, we've been we've been on YouTube now for 10 years, and every year we've opened Stadium Club, and it's always had two autographs per box, two oncard autos per box. Now, for the first time ever, they decided to tinker with it and they dropped it to one auto. So, less autos. But that's not all either. Last year, they made stadium club really nice. They beefed it up with chrome. There was chrome cards in every pack. There was chrome parallels to chase after. It was really, really nice. It was a great product and stadium club. I thought that that that I thought that was perfect. And this year they got rid of all of it. So there's no more Chrome cards at all. In fact, they even sent out an email to all of the collectors out there
